Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

    <location>~/Lookitry/openclaw/skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md</location>

  </skill>

    <name>node-connect</name>

    <location>~/Lookitry/openclaw/skills/node-connect/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>skill-creator</name>
    <description>Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills.     <location>~/Lookitry/openclaw/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>

    <name>taskflow</name>

  </skill>

compactionCheckpoints.summary:
## Goal
1. Audit and fix security issues in Lookitry SaaS project
2. Create Mission Control dashboard for agent orchestration
3. Get frontend dev server running locally to preview Mission Control

## Constraints & Preferences
- "hazlo con cuidado de no romper nada" (do it carefully without breaking anything)
- "no te dejes llevar por esas auditorias que son viejas, tienes que revisar todo de 0" (don't rely on old audits, review from scratch)
- "quiero algo mas visual, mas profesional" (want more visual, professional dashboard)
- "como puedo visualizar el mission control?" (asking how to view it)
- "lo activaste?" / "activaste el localhost?" (asking about localhost activation)
- WebWizard should help create the React version, not doing it alone

## Progress
### Done
- [x] Security audit completed: found 3 critical issues (hardcoded secrets, 878 `any` usages, XSS via iframe in blog)
- [x] Security fix 1: Changed `webhook_secret` default in `supabase-schema.sql` from `'Travis2305**_blog_n8n'` to `'CHANGE_ME_IN_ENV'`
- [x] Security fix 2: Removed hardcoded `ENTERPRISE_SYNC_TOKEN` from `docker-compose.backend.yml` (already in `.env.production`)
- [x] Security fix 3: Removed `iframe` from `ALLOWED_TAGS` and `allow/allowfullscreen` from `ALLOWED_ATTR` in `SanitizedHtml.tsx`
- [x] Security fix 4: Removed duplicate `ENV NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` in `frontend/Dockerfile`
- [x] Generated new secrets: `BLOG_WEBHOOK_SECRET=a1108af...` and `ENTERPRISE_SYNC_TOKEN=ef20401...`
- [x] Git commit: `449aba4 fix(security): remove hardcoded secrets and fix XSS vectors`
- [x] Created Mission Control skill at `/home/travis/Lookitry/openclaw/skills/mission-control/SKILL.md`
- [x] Created status script at `/home/travis/Lookitry/openclaw/skills/mission-control/scripts/status.sh`
- [x] Created project reference guide at `/home/travis/Lookitry/openclaw/skills/mission-control/references/project-guide.md`
- [x] Created HTML dashboard at `/home/travis/.openclaw/canvas/mission-control.html` (dark theme, agent cards, health grid, stats, activity feed)
- [x] Delegated to WebWizard to create React component version
- [x] WebWizard created `frontend/src/app/mission-control/page.tsx` (649 lines) + `mission-control.css`
- [x] Created API route placeholder at `frontend/src/app/api/agents/status/route.ts`
- [x] Fixed `mission-control.css` → `mission-control.module.css` rename (CSS modules don't support `@tailwind` directives)
- [x] Removed `@tailwind` directives from `mission-control.module.css`

### In Progress
- [ ] Getting frontend dev server running locally to preview Mission Control
  - Issue: `Cannot find module '@next/bundle-analyzer'` - npm isn't installing devDependencies properly despite `npm install --include=dev`
  - Issue: Tailwind/PostCSS not processing `globals.css` correctly with `@tailwind` directives
  - Issue: Middleware errors with `ENOENT: prerender-manifest.js`

### Blocked
- Frontend dev server has npm dependency issues (devDependencies not installing) that need full VPS deployment to resolve

## Key Decisions
- **Using MiniMax API key for OpenCode**: User clarified OpenCode uses the same MiniMax API key. Proceeded with explanation that MiniMax key ≠ OpenCode key (different services). User confirmed to skip OpenCode config for now.
- **Cron reminder set**: Created `SSH Credentials Reminder` cron job to remind user tomorrow at 10 AM Colombia time about Hostinger VPS SSH credentials
- **Session mode**: Using `mode="run"` (not "session") because no channel plugin registered subagent hooks
- **CSS Modules fix**: WebWizard created `mission-control.css` with `@tailwind` directives, but CSS Module imports don't process Tailwind directives → renamed to `.module.css` and removed tailwind directives

## Next Steps
1. Deploy frontend changes to VPS (production) so Mission Control can be viewed at `https://lookitry.com/mission-control`
2. Connect real data from OpenClaw to the API route (`/api/agents/status`) using `sessions_list` and `subagents` tools
3. Fix frontend dev server issues separately (not blocking production deployment)
4. Consider upgrading Next.js from 14.0.4 (has known security vulnerability per npm warning)

  - `BLOG_WEBHOOK_SECRET=a1108af730b7dc565b3d60d50ea3cdef5a3c73d66aeaf40130b07dd1cf6be2ce`
  - `ENTERPRISE_SYNC_TOKEN=ef204017cf64e604b9feeecbcd9a42d92d015ad2dd4362e793a556138bb9ffd686a07dd3dd13e2dd`

TaskFlow is the durable flow substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, ACPX, plugins, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example TaskFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>coding-agent</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.</description>
    <location>~/Lookitry/openclaw/skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>healthcheck</name>
    <description>Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).</description>
    <location>~/Lookitry/openclaw/skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>mission-control</name>
    <description>Centralized mission control for monitoring and coordinating all Lookitry sub-agents (WebWizard, DevGuardian, DataAlchemist, GrowthPilot, ArchitectAI). This skill is the nerve center for orchestrating the Lookitry SaaS project.</description>
    <location>~/Lookitry/openclaw/skills/mission-control/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>node-connect</name>
    <description>Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps.     <location>~/Lookitry/openclaw/skills/node-connect/SKILL.md</location>

Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: mission-control
resolvedSkills.description: Centralized mission control for monitoring and coordinating all Lookitry sub-agents (WebWizard, DevGuardian, DataAlchemist, GrowthPilot, ArchitectAI). Use when the user asks for a "dashboard", "mission control", "control center", "agent status", "monitor all agents", "coordination panel", or wants to check the status of all agents, delegate tasks to specific agents, or get an overview of the entire Lookitry project. This skill is the nerve center for orchestrating the Lookitry SaaS project.

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow
resolvedSkills.description: Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. TaskFlow is the durable flow substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, ACPX, plugins, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example TaskFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.
